response:
Nurse Emily organized a creative writing workshop for the 24 residents in her care facility. She divided them into groups of 6, resulting in 4 groups in total. To ensure an engaging session, Emily prepared 3 writing prompts for each group, totaling 12 prompts. 

In addition, Emily anticipated that the groups might want to continue writing and had prepared an extra set of 10 prompts to be shared equally among all the groups. This means each group will receive 2.5 extra prompts, which will be handled by rounding or distributing as needed. 

Emily also made sure to keep a well-stocked library of writing materials and referenced the facility’s library for additional resources. The library was an essential part of the workshop setup, providing a quiet and inspiring space for the residents to express themselves. 

In total, Emily needs to have 12 initial prompts plus 10 extra prompts, which equals 22 writing prompts for the session. 

The END.
